blockingIssues.description: `build_training_dataset()` still leaves `weather_fetcher` optional, and all shipped callers rebuild training data without passing one, so the default training/model/backtest path continues to emit neutral weather constants instead of actual t14-derived weather features.

`_fill_missing_feature_values()` backfills NaNs with full-dataset column means after the full 2019-2025 dataset is assembled, rather than using Marcel or league-average fallbacks at feature-generation time; this misses the stated fill strategy and can leak future information into earlier rows.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/workspace-state.md` to replace the stale note that the training-data builder avoids team game logs; the current builder now threads `team_logs_fetcher` through offense, defense, and bullpen feature rebuilds.
Extended `.factory/library/ml-calibration.md` to record that the `python -m` module-alias / `__module__` pinning convention also applies to stacking bundles, not just calibration bundles.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Explicitly direct Windows workers to the repo's Windows-native setup and validation commands (or direct `.venv\Scripts\python.exe` invocations) instead of shell-specific examples, since multiple ML workers still needed justified deviations during setup and syntax-check steps.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reviews for `fix-t20-walkforward-rebuild` and earlier ml-pipeline fixes both cite justified Windows-specific deviations even though `.factory/services.yaml` already publishes Windows-native command variants.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a shared rule that when a verified default changes for a cross-cutting ML stage (such as calibration), downstream mission guidance and milestone briefs must be updated in the same round so reviewers and implementers are not working from stale defaults.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `fix-t20-calibration-parity` resolved a real drift caused in part by stale mission text that still described an isotonic-calibrated stack while the live code and library now use Platt as the default calibration path.

rejectedObservations.observation: The claim that `.factory/services.yaml` `typecheck_windows` is unreliable in this exec environment.
rejectedObservations.reason: retested locally during synthesis with the exact manifest command and it exited 0, so this was not reproducible evidence for a shared-state bug

rejectedObservations.observation: The repeated `init_windows` quoting bug observation from `.factory/services.yaml`.
rejectedObservations.reason: already captured in prior scrutiny rounds, and fixing it would require overwriting an existing shared manifest entry rather than making an additive update during synthesis
